formulas.name: The Domino Theory
formulas.formula: If one country in Southeast Asia falls to communism, others will follow.
formulas.notes: A policy statement by US President Dwight D. Eisenhower, explaining the fear of communist expansion in Southeast Asia during the Cold War.

formulas.name: The Truman Doctrine
formulas.formula: The United States will provide economic and military aid to countries threatened by communism.
formulas.notes: A policy statement by US President Harry S. Truman, outlining US foreign policy during the Cold War, emphasizing the need to contain communism and promote democracy.

formulas.name: The Marshall Plan
formulas.formula: The United States will provide economic aid to war-torn Europe to rebuild and prevent communist expansion.
formulas.notes: A plan proposed by US Secretary of State George Marshall in 1947, aimed at rebuilding war-torn Europe and preventing the spread of communism.

formulas.name: Containment Policy
formulas.formula: The policy of preventing the spread of communism through diplomatic, economic, and military means.
formulas.notes: A key aspect of US foreign policy during the Cold War, aiming to prevent the spread of communism and promote democracy.

formulas.name: The Soviet Union's Five-Year Plans
formulas.formula: The Soviet Union will rapidly industrialize and modernize through a series of five-year plans.
formulas.notes: A series of economic plans implemented by the Soviet Union from 1928 to 1990, aimed at rapid industrialization and modernization.

formulas.name: The Warsaw Pact
formulas.formula: A military alliance between Soviet-aligned Eastern European countries.
formulas.notes: A military alliance formed in 1955 by Soviet-aligned Eastern European countries, in response to the formation of NATO.

formulas.name: The Non-Aligned Movement
formulas.formula: A group of countries that sought to remain neutral in the Cold War, avoiding allegiance to either the US or the Soviet Union.
formulas.notes: A movement formed in 1961 by countries that sought to avoid taking sides in the Cold War, promoting non-alignment and neutrality.
